summary: All 8 scenarios FAIL: https://mint.minibits.cash/Bitcoin returns HTTP 404 JSON {"detail":"Not Found"} — no HTML UI is served at this URL path, making all functional tests impossible.

results.actual: Navigation to 'https://mint.minibits.cash/Bitcoin/login or auth page' (URL with spaces, percent-encoded) returned {"detail":"Not Found"}. No login form, email input, password input, or any UI elements found. Page title is empty.
issues: Auth URL path is invalid — contains spaces which were percent-encoded, producing a non-existent route
Underlying base path /Bitcoin already returns 404; no auth UI exists at this host+path combination
Zero HTML elements found: no form, no inputs, no buttons, no heading
fix_actions: Verify the correct authentication URL for mint.minibits.cash — the /Bitcoin sub-path does not exist; check API docs or the mint's NUT-06 info endpoint (typically GET /v1/info or GET /info)
If mint.minibits.cash serves a web UI, confirm the correct path (may be https://mint.minibits.cash/ without the /Bitcoin suffix)
Update test scenario step 1 URL — remove the literal text 'or auth page' which was appended to the URL string during test generation
results.evidence: page_text_snippet: '{"detail":"Not Found"}'; page_title: ''; all 16 element types: false; navigated URL: https://mint.minibits.cash/Bitcoin/login%20or%20auth%20page

results.actual: https://mint.minibits.cash/Bitcoin/ returned a JSON 404 response body {"detail":"Not Found"} with empty page title. No HTML content, no UI elements of any kind were found. Page load completed but served only a raw API error response, not an HTML application.
issues: URL https://mint.minibits.cash/Bitcoin/ returns HTTP 404 JSON — not an HTML page
Page title is empty string — confirms no HTML document was loaded
All 16 UI element categories (header, nav, form, button, link, image, heading, etc.) are absent
fix_actions: Confirm the correct base URL for the minibits.cash Cashu mint web UI — the /Bitcoin path is not resolving; try https://mint.minibits.cash/ directly
Check if the /Bitcoin route was renamed or removed in a recent deployment by reviewing the server routing config or nginx/caddy virtual host configuration at mint.minibits.cash
If this is a Cashu NUT-06 API endpoint, verify the correct path (e.g., GET /v1/info) and update test targets accordingly — a REST API endpoint is not testable as a web UI
results.evidence: page_text_snippet: '{"detail":"Not Found"}'; page_url: 'https://mint.minibits.cash/Bitcoin/'; page_title: ''; all elements_found: false; page load step succeeded (TCP connection made) but content is 404 JSON

results.actual: Page returned 404 JSON — no HTML or JavaScript was loaded. console_errors array is empty only because no JavaScript executed, not because the app is error-free. The empty console is a symptom of the 404 failure, not evidence of health.
issues: Cannot meaningfully test for JS console errors when the page fails to load HTML/JS at all
The 404 JSON response means the JavaScript bundle was never fetched or executed
Empty console_errors is misleading — it reflects a non-existent application, not a working one
fix_actions: Fix the root 404 at https://mint.minibits.cash/Bitcoin/ (see page_loads fix_actions) — this is a prerequisite before console error testing is valid
After restoring the correct URL, re-run the console_errors scenario to capture actual JavaScript execution errors
Check server-side logs at mint.minibits.cash for the /Bitcoin route to determine if a deployment or routing regression caused the 404
results.evidence: page_text_snippet: '{"detail":"Not Found"}'; console_errors: [] (empty due to no JS execution, not a clean bill of health); page_title: ''; all elements: false

results.actual: Page returned 404 JSON at https://mint.minibits.cash/Bitcoin/. Viewport resize was not performed (step reported current viewport as 800x600, did not resize to 375px). No layout elements exist to test responsiveness on.
issues: No HTML UI loaded — cannot test responsive layout on a 404 JSON response
Viewport resize step was not executed — only reported current viewport dimensions
Zero layout elements found: no header, nav, footer, heading, main content
fix_actions: Restore the application at https://mint.minibits.cash/Bitcoin/ before responsive layout testing is possible
Fix the viewport resize step in the test automation — the step reported existing viewport rather than resizing to 375px mobile width
After 404 is fixed, verify CSS media queries and flex/grid layouts render correctly at 375px viewport width
results.evidence: page_text_snippet: '{"detail":"Not Found"}'; viewport step detail: '{"width":800,"height":600}' (no resize performed); all layout elements: false

results.actual: Page returned 404 JSON. Navigation click step explicitly failed with 'Could not determine click target from step text'. No nav, link, button, or header elements found on the page.
issues: Click step failed: 'Could not determine click target from step text'
No link elements found on the page (elements_found.link: false)
No nav or navigation elements found — 404 JSON response has no HTML structure
fix_actions: Fix the root 404 at https://mint.minibits.cash/Bitcoin/ before navigation testing is valid

Update test automation click step to specify a concrete CSS selector or aria-label rather than natural language ('Click on main navigation links') to prevent 'Could not determine click target' failures
After URL is fixed, verify <nav> and <a> elements are present in the rendered HTML and check for broken href values or missing route handlers
results.evidence: page_text_snippet: '{"detail":"Not Found"}'; click step success: false, detail: 'Could not determine click target from step text'; elements_found.nav: false; elements_found.link: false

results.actual: Page returned 404 JSON. No form, input, or button elements found on the page. The 'Find any forms' verification step confirmed zero forms present.
issues: No form elements found on page (elements_found.form: false)
No input elements found (elements_found.input: false)
No buttons found (elements_found.button: false)
Page is serving 404 JSON, not an HTML application with interactive forms
fix_actions: Fix the root 404 at https://mint.minibits.cash/Bitcoin/ — no form testing is possible on a 404 response
After URL is fixed, confirm the Cashu mint web UI renders form elements (e.g., token redemption form, minting amount input)
Verify the frontend JavaScript bundle is loading correctly and rendering dynamic form components (check for React/Vue hydration errors after URL is fixed)
results.evidence: page_text_snippet: '{"detail":"Not Found"}'; elements_found.form: false; elements_found.input: false; elements_found.button: false; 'Find any forms' step result: 'Element check: {"form": False}'

results.actual: Page returned 404 JSON. No image elements found on the page. Both image-checking steps confirmed elements_found.image: false.
issues: No image elements found (elements_found.image: false on both verification steps)
Page is a JSON 404 response — no HTML document, no or
Cannot verify image load status on a non-HTML page
fix_actions: Fix the root 404 at https://mint.minibits.cash/Bitcoin/ before image testing is valid
After URL is fixed, check for broken image src paths — verify CDN or static asset URLs return 200 and are not blocked by CORS or CSP headers
Audit network requests for image assets returning 4xx/5xx after the page loads correctly
results.evidence: page_text_snippet: '{"detail":"Not Found"}'; elements_found.image: false (confirmed by two separate verification steps); no network_failures recorded (no images were even requested)

results.actual: Page returned 404 JSON. Time to first content and responsiveness cannot be meaningfully measured on a 404 error response. The 'Note time to first content' step was not automated. The page technically loaded quickly — but it loaded an error, not the application.
issues: 404 JSON response means application content never loaded — performance metrics are irrelevant
Time-to-first-content step was not automated: 'Step not automatable via CDP'
No UI elements loaded, so 'responsiveness' of interactions cannot be assessed
fix_actions: Fix the root 404 at https://mint.minibits.cash/Bitcoin/ before performance testing is valid
After URL is fixed, implement performance timing using CDP Performance domain metrics (document.timing, LCP, FID) rather than manual observation
If page load time exceeds 3 seconds after fix, profile network waterfall for large JS bundles or slow API calls and consider code splitting or lazy loading
results.evidence: page_text_snippet: '{"detail":"Not Found"}'; performance timing step: 'Step not automatable via CDP'; page verified as 'has content: True' but content is only the 404 JSON string

critical_failures: auth_flow: Auth URL returns 404 Not Found — no authentication UI exists at https://mint.minibits.cash/Bitcoin/login
page_loads: Main URL https://mint.minibits.cash/Bitcoin/ returns HTTP 404 JSON {"detail":"Not Found"} — application is not serving HTML
console_errors: Cannot verify JS health — page 404 means JavaScript never executed
recommendations: IMMEDIATE: Investigate why https://mint.minibits.cash/Bitcoin/ returns 404 — check server routing config, recent deployments, and DNS/proxy settings for mint.minibits.cash
Verify the correct URL structure for the minibits.cash Cashu mint — if it is a pure REST API (NUT-06), test scenarios should target API endpoints (e.g., GET /v1/info, POST /v1/mint) not a web UI
If a web UI is expected, check whether the /Bitcoin path was recently renamed (e.g., to just / or /app) and update test target URLs accordingly
Re-run the full test suite after the 404 is resolved — all 8 scenarios need fresh evidence before any PASS verdicts can be issued
Fix test automation for the responsive_layout scenario — viewport resize step is not executing (reporting current 800x600 instead of resizing to 375px)
Fix navigation_works click step — use explicit CSS selectors instead of natural language descriptions to avoid 'Could not determine click target' failures
